title: Dependent Co-Arising
definition: The Buddhist concept that everything is dependent and interconnected, arising and passing away due to causes and conditions.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The Buddhist concept that everything in the universe is interconnected and nothing exists independently.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A key tenet of Buddhism, Dependent Co-Arising (Pratītyasamutpāda) explains the arising and passing away of phenomena due to causes and conditions.
difficulty_levels.expert: The realization that all phenomena are transient and empty of a permanent self or substantial essence, leading to the cessation of suffering.

extra_fields.insight: The realization that all phenomena are transient and empty of a permanent self or substantial essence, leading to the cessation of suffering.
extra_fields.historical_context: A central concept in Buddhism that emphasizes interconnectedness and impermanence.
limitations: Dependent Co-Arising is a philosophical framework and not empirically testable.
applications: Meditative practices for understanding the nature of reality.
Influence on Buddhist ethics and morality.
